channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How to put down your phone in 2025
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: People who visit adult or gambling websites double their risk of malware
item.description: Visiting adult and gambling websites doubles the risk of inadvertently installing malware onto work devices, according to a new study. Fabio Massacci at the University of Trento and Vrije University in Amsterdam presented the findings of a massive analysis of real-life data from around the world.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: 14 new California laws to watch for in 2025
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom and lawmakers passed big changes to laws this year, and many go into effect in 2025. Here's a list of some of the most impactful.
item.dc:creator: Grant Stringer, The Mercury News

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy S25 series just leaked again – here are the top 4 rumored details
item.description: We're hearing even more about the four new models expected to make up the Galaxy S25 series next year.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: La falta de métricas de éxito de los CIO condena a muchos proyectos de IA
item.description: Según la firma de investigación IDC, muchas organizaciones han lanzado docenas de proyectos de prueba de concepto de inteligencia artificial (IA) solo para ver que un gran porcentaje fracasa, en parte porque los CIO no saben si las POC cumplen con las métricas clave.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: New 2025 laws hit hot topics from AI in movies to rapid-fire guns
item.description: New laws taking effect with the new year will affect Hollywood actors, social media stars and chatty motorists.
item.dc:creator: David A. Lieb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Cyberattack hits Japan Airlines, delaying flights for holiday travelers
item.description: Japan Airlines said it was hit by a cyberattack Thursday, causing delays to more than 20 domestic flights but the carrier said it was able to stop the onslaught and restore its systems hours later. There was no impact on flight safety, it said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Samsung pulls curtains on classified operation called Project Infinity, where teams compete relentlessly to improve security on billions of Galaxy phones
item.description: Samsung's Project Infinity, featuring Red, Blue, Purple teams, and the CTI taskforce, protects Galaxy users from cyber threats.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy defend Silicon Valley's foreign-born hires
item.description: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy, co-chairs of President-elect Trump’s new “Department of Government Efficiency,” are defending the tech industry’s reliance on foreign-born engineers as the incoming Trump administration prepares to crack down on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: YouTube has a new plan to combat clickbait
item.description: Thumbnails play the YouTube equivalent of a movie poster, aiming to draw your attention to click and watch when you have hundreds of videos clogging your recommended content.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Network problems delay flights at two oneworld Alliance airlines
item.description: IT problems struck flights at two oneworld Alliance member airlines this week, leading to delays and flight cancellations. Network equipment connecting internal and external systems at Japan Airlines (JAL) malfunctioned early on Dec.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Leveraging Avaya Experience Platform to accelerate your digital banking transformation
item.description: Banks are striving for digital innovation but regulatory constraints, data security and privacy concerns, integration challenges, and the high costs of enabling change prevent 70% from achieving their transformation goals.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Samsung tipped to unveil the Galaxy Ring 2 at its January Unpacked event
item.description: It looks as though there are going to be plenty of new gadgets to talk about at the January 22 showcase.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: What is an IT consultant? Roles, types, salaries, and how to become one
item.description: The IT function within organizations has become far more complex in recent years. Enterprise infrastructures have expanded far beyond the traditional ones focused on company-owned and -operated data centers.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Japan Airlines was hit by a cyberattack, delaying flights during the year-end holiday season
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan Airlines said it was hit by a cyberattack Thursday, causing delays to more than 20 domestic flights but the carrier said it was able to stop the onslaught and restore its systems hours later. There was no impact on flight safety, it said.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Undersea power cable linking Finland and Estonia hit by outage, prompting investigation
item.description: FRANKFURT, Germany (AP) — A power cable linking Finland and Estonia under the Baltic Sea suffered an outage, prompting an investigation, Finnish Prime Minister Petteri Orpo said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The top Windows PC tips of 2024
item.description: The end of the year means it’s time to reflect on what’s really important in life: friends, family — and Windows PC tips. That may sound silly, but I’ve always believed it’s true.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Python in 2024: Faster, more powerful, and more popular than ever
item.description: Over the course of 2024, Python has proven again and again why it’s one of the most popular, useful, and promising programming languages out there.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What is serverless? Serverless computing explained
item.description: Over the past two decades, considerable innovation has gone into making sure developers don’t have to think much about the underlying physical machine their code runs on. Serverless computing is a paradigm that takes this objective to its logical conclusion.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Navy confronting hurdles to implementing 5G-enabled technologies at scale
item.description: 5G makes exciting emerging technologies possible, but first the Department of the Navy has to solve spectrum integration, cybersecurity and acquisition hurdles. The post Navy confronting hurdles to implementing 5G-enabled technologies at scale first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Timeshare terror leaves retired couple $50K in the hole after being scammed
item.description: The FBI is warning timeshare owners of a telemarketing scam tied to a Mexican drug cartel. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson provides steps to stay safe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Baby mammoth preserved for 50,000 years is unveiled in Russia's Siberia
item.description: The 50,000-year-old remains of a baby mammoth uncovered by melting permafrost have been unveiled to the public by researchers in Russia’s Siberia region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: WinGet: The best way to keep Windows apps updated
item.description: For more than two years, I’ve been working with WinGet daily to monitor and maintain the apps on my Windows 10 and 11 PCs. For those not already in the know, WinGet is the built-in, command-line interface to Microsoft’s Windows Package Manager service.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Los clientes de SAP están tardando en implantar la IA de forma generalizada
item.description: Una nueva encuesta realizada por SAP a organizaciones clientes de las soluciones de la compañía muestra que, a pesar de experimentar con la IA, pocas han implementado esta tecnología (incuyendo la modalidad generativa) en sus empresas.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The AI backlash couldn’t have come at a better time
item.description: At a developers conference I attended not too long ago, attendees did little to hide their disdain every time the term “AI” was bandied about.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Ford to donate $1M, fleet of vehicles to Trump inauguration
item.description: Ford Motor Co. said it will donate $1 million and a small fleet of vehicles for use in President-elect Trump’s inauguration, multiple outlets reported Monday. Ford joins a recent wave of major tech companies that have pledged hefty donations to the incoming president’s inaugural fund.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Trump administration’s AI team comes into focus, as agencies reach 1,700 AI use cases
item.description: Federal CIO Clare Martorana says the Trump administration should consider a shared-services model to accelerate the use of AI across agencies. The post Trump administration’s AI team comes into focus, as agencies reach 1,700 AI use cases first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: I tried out 3 media outlets’ AI chatbots. Here’s what happened
item.description: How fitting for Time to debut an AI chatbot last week with its annual Person of the Year package. AI was almost certainly Thing of the Year, after all, even if there’s no official award for that.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Scammers exploited mom’s fears to steal her entire life's savings
item.description: Rising elder scams: How to recognize them and protect yourself and your loved ones. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you outsmart the scammers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The exciting new world of Redis
item.description: If you’re a developer who wants the most feature-rich, high-performance version of Redis, your choice is clear: Redis and not a fork. If you have the time and inclination to dabble in ideological debates about open source licensing, well, you might make another choice.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nissan and Honda to attempt a merger that would create the world's No. 3 automaker
item.description: Japanese automakers Nissan and Honda have announced plans to work toward a merger that would catapult them to a top position in an industry in the midst of tectonic shifts as it transitions away from its reliance on fossil fuels.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi And Elaine Kurtenbach, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump pushes back on 'President Musk' claims
item.description: President-elect Trump on Sunday rejected the recent dubbing of tech billionaire Elon Musk as “President Musk” by Democrats. “No, he’s not gonna be president, that I can tell you,” Trump said at conservative group Turning Point's “America Fest” in Phoenix, Ariz.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Top 5 mistakes that could expose your financial data to cybercriminals
item.description: There are common habits and oversights that could leave your financial information vulnerable to cybercriminals, according to Kurt the CyberGuy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NASA's Parker Solar Probe aims to fly closer to the sun like never before
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A NASA spacecraft aims to fly closer to the sun than any object sent before. The Parker Solar Probe was launched in 2018 to get a close-up look at the sun. Since then, it has flown straight through the sun's corona: the outer atmosphere visible during a total solar eclipse.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, KREX 5
